ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for deep-fried beancurd skin rolls with pork filling; closest match is tofu skin roll food product (FOODON_03315595).
ontology.foodex2_description: Tofu and tofu products — used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for beancurd skin rolls, a processed soy-based dish.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for deep-fried beancurd skin rolls; A0FKP (tofu and tofu products) is the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for beancurd skin rolls as a prepared dish; SNOMED CT coverage of composite Asian prepared foods is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of beancurd skin rolls is approximately 2 rolls (about 100g), commonly consumed as a snack or side dish in a Chinese meal.

nutrient_source.title: Beancurd skin rolls — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Beancurd skin rolls (CR ID: F09050002). Iron value is reported as -1 (not available) in the source record. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, potassium, and micronutrients not reported by HPB for this entry; set to -1 per numeric hygiene rules.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Soy-Based Foods — Analogous estimate based on tofu and soy protein products (GI ~32) from the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for deep-fried beancurd skin rolls. GI estimated at approximately 32 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for tofu and soy protein-based foods, which consistently show low GI values (GI 15–40) due to high protein and fat content slowing glucose absorption. Carbohydrate content is only 3.16g/100g, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 1.0 per 100g serving — clinically negligible.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium at 549mg/100g with minimal potassium and fibre offset
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit or avoid beancurd skin rolls due to sodium content of 549mg/100g, which approaches 25% of the recommended daily sodium limit (2000mg) in a single 100g serving; request steamed versions and reduced-sodium soy sauce to lower sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (3.16g/100g) and low GI (~32); high protein supports glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Beancurd skin rolls have a very low carbohydrate content of 3.16g/100g and an estimated low GI of approximately 32, making them suitable for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes; however, portion control is advised given the high fat content of 14.46g/100g, which may contribute to insulin resistance if consumed in excess.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (14.46g/100g) from deep-frying and high sodium (549mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The deep-frying process elevates total fat content to 14.46g/100g and sodium to 549mg/100g, both of which are risk factors for cardiovascular disease; patients should prefer steamed beancurd skin rolls and limit portion size to reduce saturated fat and sodium intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and potential trans fat formation; high sodium exacerbates tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried beancurd skin rolls as the frying process generates pro-inflammatory AGEs and potential trans fats; steamed versions are strongly preferred to minimise joint inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beancurd skin rolls (腐皮卷, fu pi juan) are a popular Chinese and Singaporean dish consisting of dried tofu skin (yuba) wrapped around a savoury filling of minced pork and diced parsley, then deep-fried until golden and crispy. The outer beancurd skin becomes light and crunchy when fried, while the interior remains moist and flavourful from the seasoned pork filling. They are commonly served as a snack, dim sum item, or side dish in Chinese hawker centres and restaurants across Singapore and Southern China. Nutritionally, per 100g they provide 199 kcal, 14g protein, 14.46g fat, and only 3.16g carbohydrate, making them a high-protein, low-carbohydrate food. Sodium is elevated at 549mg/100g due to soy sauce seasoning in the filling. The low carbohydrate content and high protein make them suitable for blood glucose management, but the high sodium and fat from deep-frying raise concerns for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. Calcium is present at 41mg/100g from the beancurd skin. Iron data is not available in the HPB source. Steamed versions are clinically preferable for patients managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis.
common_user_queries: Are beancurd skin rolls suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID: F09050002), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. The food identity is exact. GI is estimated from an analogous soy-based food in the University of Sydney GI Database, which is the approved GI source; this introduces minor uncertainty but does not reduce overall confidence given the negligible carbohydrate content.
notes: iron_mg is set to -1: the HPB Singapore Foodo database does not report iron for this specific entry (F09050002 Beancurd skin rolls); no alternative approved source was available to supply this value. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this traditional deep-fried preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. GI is an analogous estimate (~32, low) based on tofu and soy protein foods from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for deep-fried beancurd skin rolls was found in any approved source. The dietary tag 'plant_based' from the input has been removed as the filling contains minced pork. The 'keto' tag has been retained as a cultural/dietary flag given the very low carbohydrate content. Halal status is marked false as pork is a primary ingredient.
